mapping.a_element: Ising-like model on a square lattice with neighbor preferences (Schelling-Ising model)
Inhomogeneous or self-organized temperature T as a control parameter for dynamics
mapping.b_element: Effective temperatures or dynamical heterogeneities in KCMs arising from kinetic constraints

mapping.a_element: Segregation as a collective phenomenon emerging from local interactions
mapping.b_element: Glassy dynamics and slow relaxation due to kinetic constraints in KCMs

mapping.a_element: Feedback between segregation and temperature leading to self-organization
mapping.b_element: Non-equilibrium stationary states or aging dynamics in KCMs under external driving

brief_justification: While both papers involve Ising-like models and temperature-like parameters, the Schelling-Ising model focuses on equilibrium-like segregation driven by neighbor preferences, whereas KCMs emphasize non-equilibrium glassy dynamics due to kinetic constraints. The core mechanisms—preference-driven segregation vs. constraint-driven slow dynamics—are fundamentally different, despite superficial similarities in lattice-based modeling.
